title: Misatos feelings towards Shinji at the start of Q
display_title: Misatos feelings towards Shinji at the start of Q
wikitext:
[]: Misato's feelings towards Shinji at the start of Q             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue May 20, 2014 4:29 am (This is a continuation of a conversation in the wrong topic, as linked from the quote below - a mod might like to bring over the earlier messages that fit here.) ElMariachi wrote: Mind putting a scan of the relevant page? But in a more appropriate topic, I think we're kinda derailing that one.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue May 20, 2014 4:33 am ^ I think I see contempt and hurt in her eyes to be honest. I know she has other emotions for him but that's what I see at that moment, maybe a wee bit indifference too.

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Tue May 20, 2014 4:48 am I see worrying she is worried as a mother who see his son derailling shit

[]: Re: Misato's feelings towards Shinji at the start of Q             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 20, 2014 5:07 am pwhodges wrote: Here are the frames for the earlier scene discussed: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Tue May 20, 2014 6:21 am It's so rare to see facial expression animated to begin with, and then you can read so many things from it in Q. You just gotta love the facial animation in Q. This kinda reminds me of the subtle movings of Ritsuko's head in Episode 23, shortly before her breakdown. You can see that she's about to lose her cool. Love it. In this particular case I'd say Misato seems concerned, troubled. Conflicted between what she ought to do as a crew leader and what she wants to as a human being. But most of all I can see extreme emotional pressure until the moment she puts her glasses off. Then it looks like utter contempt and arrogance.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue May 20, 2014 7:13 am ChaddyManPrime wrote: ^ I think I see contempt and hurt in her eyes to be honest. I know she has other emotions for him but that's what I see at that moment, maybe a wee bit indifference too.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 20, 2014 7:50 am Crazy theory (not that much) : Shinji just requested to pilot again to go help Asuka . So what if Misato decided to use a disdainful look and venomous voice to remove his desire to help, since after the fallout with his father after Bardiel, helping is obviously the only thing that incite him to pilot (after all he fought Zeruel mainly to save Rei), so it would definitively remove his desire to pilot the Eva. Of course that would also antagonize him, but she probably thought that it was an acceptable price to pay for mankind's safety. And it did worked alright, he very quickly lost all trust in them and wanted nothing to do with them (which included to pilot the Eva to help), except Misato couldn't imagine that neo-NERV would be here to "save" him, making her little strategy to discourage Shinji from piloting horribly backfire on her when he happily ditched them to go with "Rei".

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Tue May 20, 2014 8:32 am The way I interpret it is that Misato still cares about Shinji to some degree but given her rank & position now she can't let her emotions interfere with her work and thus hides her true feelings.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue May 20, 2014 9:46 am ^ Oh no doubt Good Sir, I think that's the only reason Shinji's head isn't in a million tiny pieces right now.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue May 20, 2014 10:29 am I definitely don't see contempt in the first one. It looks more like a little bit of surprise, and then moving on to "Shit. He's really here, isn't he." Just my two cents on that. As for the second one, the face drawing definitely looks like it holds contempt, the but larger image looks different. Misato really doesn't look like she wants to be playing this role, indicative of her later reluctance to blow off Shinji's noggin with the DSS choker.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 20, 2014 11:45 am Just to precise : the two images represent two different scenes : The first one is when Misato is asking to Ritsuko if the boy down there is really Shinji Ikari (just before Ritsuko gives her the command for the DSS Choker) : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue May 20, 2014 11:57 am Yeah, I remember. I had the BD open when I was writing.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue May 20, 2014 1:23 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I definitely don't see contempt in the first one. It looks more like a little bit of surprise, and then moving on to "Shit. He's really here, isn't he." Just my two cents on that. As for the second one, the face drawing definitely looks like it holds contempt, the but larger image looks different. Misato really doesn't look like she wants to be playing this role, indicative of her later reluctance to blow off Shinji's noggin with the DSS choker.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue May 20, 2014 3:03 pm There's definitely a change in her expression in the second spoiler. There is indeed contempt, but I'm more interested in the expression that comes before it.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue May 20, 2014 3:08 pm Maybe disgust or conviction? The eyes and mouth without a face kind of reminds me of a face that a stereotypical Tsundere would make when talking, so maybe annoyance? The picture that is next to it is the one with contempt, to me at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue May 20, 2014 3:22 pm I'm of the impression that the full face came first, then followed by the drawing of just the eyes and mouth. I think the first one (full face) is more... you know, maybe it's disappointment. Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue May 20, 2014 3:29 pm Meh, no idea bro, I think I'll stick with contempt for the full face though.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue May 20, 2014 7:14 pm Watched the part of the third pic on the BD. She looks sad, but she's hiding it. Isn't escaping from Shinji what Gendo did? But he didn't want to hurt him. Misato is turning into a Gendo. But really, we need some Instrumentality in 4.0 to really know what's going on in her head. Misato's change is one of the things that hurts the most in 3.0. I love this film.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue May 20, 2014 10:03 pm Misato's going to have a great heart-breaking moment in Final where she opens up to Shinji a.la End of Evangelion. I'll be in tears. We'll be in tears. It'll be magical. Misato's coldness in 3.0 has never bothered me because you can tell from the way she's animated & voiced (in Japanese not the misguided dubs) that there's A LOT of emotions bubbling under the surface. I'm positive it's one of the few things I can positivly feel will be in Final.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed May 28, 2014 12:31 am Here's what I see in those faces; pain, hurt, sorrow, contempt, discomfort, disgust, indifference, sympathy, pity, and recognition.
